behavior_summary: Provides the gameplay logic for a reagent/jar-like item family: inspecting (U8P_ETHEREAG::look) maps internal frame values to human-readable reagent/item names and displays them to the player; using (use) may apply an effect via an external routine and can consume/change the item's frame based on random rolls (eventually becoming an empty jar); entering and leaving a proximity (fast) area spawns and cleans up an associated transient effect/process and normalizes stored frame/state values.

Frame values encode subtype/state (different reagents, jar contents, or empty jar). The script uses Item::getFrame / Item::setFrame to read and write that state and branches on frame ranges and specific frames to decide messaging, state changes, and proximity behavior.

Proximity handlers (enterFastArea/leaveFastArea) tie the physical presence of an actor to spawning a short-lived process/effect (FREE::1C19) and perform cleanup (FREE::2A56) on exit; they also normalize or remap certain frame values when the area is entered.
notable_control_flow: U8P_ETHEREAG::look: switch-like branching on Item::getFrame() with explicit mappings: frame==1 -> "eye of newt "; frame==2 or 3 -> "bat wing "; frame in 4..9 -> "snake skin "; frame in 10..16 -> "dragon blood " (with special cases frames 11/12 -> "jar of newt eyes ", frame==13 -> "jar ").

U8P_ETHEREAG::use: guards out frames 11 and 12 (no further action there), otherwise reads item position, generates randoms (urandom) to compute a value passed to FREE::24A7, then with another random roll may increment the item's frame (consumption/aging); if frame==13 barks "empty ".

U8P_ETHEREAG::enterFastArea: read-and-normalize frame value (e.g., remap frame 16 to 10 and increment frames in 10..15), spawn FREE::1C19 for the player with code 02, then write possibly-updated frame back via Item::setFrame.
U8P_ETHEREAG::leaveFastArea: invoke FREE::2A56 with the fast-area info/handle to undo or notify about the spawned proximity effect.
open_questions: Exact semantics of FREE::24A7 called by U8P_ETHEREAG::use — it is inferred to apply an effect or lookup based on item type and a random-derived value, but the precise behavior is unknown.
Precise meaning of each numeric frame value beyond human-readable strings (e.g., whether ranges represent different qualities, stack counts, or grouped reagent types).
The exact content and lifecycle of the process/effect created by FREE::1C19 (visual, sound, or mechanic) and how it uses the pid and code arguments.
Whether other scripts or engine systems mutate the same frame values concurrently (possible race/consistency considerations when normalizing frames on enterFastArea).

main_function.name: U8P_ETHEREAG::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the 'U8P_ETHEREAG::look' (examine) event: read the item's frame and present a context-appropriate reagent or jar description string to the player via Item::bark.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_ETHEREAG::look) invoked when a player or actor examines the item.

function_map.name: U8P_ETHEREAG::look
function_map.role: Map item frame/state to a human-readable reagent/jar description and emit it to the viewer (Item::bark).

function_map.name: U8P_ETHEREAG::use
function_map.role: Handle activation/use of the item: possibly apply an effect (FREE::24A7), perform random-based consumption/state change (increment frame), and bark 'empty ' when appropriate.

function_map.name: U8P_ETHEREAG::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Proximity entry handler: normalize/adjust frame state, spawn a proximity effect/process (FREE::1C19) for the entering player, and persist any frame changes.

function_map.name: U8P_ETHEREAG::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Proximity exit handler: call cleanup/notification routine (FREE::2A56) to undo or deregister the proximity effect/process.

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: Engine-provided accessor returning the item's frame/state identifier.

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: Engine-provided mutator for the item's frame/state.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Emit a short text/message for the item to a player/process.

function_map.name: FREE::24A7
function_map.role: External routine invoked with item type and a random-derived value; likely applies an effect or resolves type-dependent behavior.

function_map.name: FREE::1C19
function_map.role: Spawn or create a transient process/effect associated with proximity (used in enterFastArea).

function_map.name: FREE::2A56
function_map.role: Cleanup/notification routine called when leaving a fast area to undo or cancel the spawned effect/process.

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Random number generator used to determine consumption/behavior on use.

function_map.name: Item::getX
function_map.role: Return item X coordinate (used by use to collect position context).

function_map.name: Item::getY
function_map.role: Return item Y coordinate (used by use to collect position context).

function_map.name: Item::getZ
function_map.role: Return item Z (stack/level) coordinate (used by use).

function_map.name: Item::getType
function_map.role: Return the item type identifier (passed to FREE::24A7).

internal_calls.purpose: Read the item's frame/state to decide which description string to show.
Display the selected reagent/jar description to the player/viewer.
Inspect current state/frame to decide behavior and whether to proceed with use logic.
Obtain item X coordinate for contextual/randomized effect computation.
Obtain item Y coordinate for contextual/randomized effect computation.
Obtain item Z coordinate for contextual/randomized effect computation.
Read and then normalize or remap frame state before spawning proximity effects.
Persist the possibly-updated/normalized frame state back to the item.
Notify/cleanup the proximity effect/process associated with the fast-area handle.

external_calls.purpose: Produce random values used both to compute an argument for FREE::24A7 and to decide whether to increment the item's frame (consume).
Apply or compute an effect based on the item's type and a random-derived value (inferred to be an effect/resolution routine).
Increment the item's frame to represent consumption/usage when random roll indicates.
Spawn a proximity-associated process/effect for the entering player (pid) with code 02 and arg 0 (likely visual/audio/interaction).
Emit a text string describing the reagent/jar to the player.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides the gameplay logic for a reagent/jar-like item family: inspecting (U8P_ETHEREAG::look) maps internal frame values to human-readable reagent/item names and displays them to the player; using (use) may apply an effect via an external routine and can consume/change the item's frame based on random rolls (eventually becoming an empty jar); entering and leaving a proximity (fast) area spawns and cleans up an associated transient effect/process and normalizes stored frame/state values.
